messages.content: You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the title of the standard shown on this page?
Acceptability of Electronic Assemblies.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which organization developed the standard shown on this page?
Association Connecting Electronics Industries.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
According to the IPC position statement, is use of a newly published revision automatically required in an existing customer-supplier relationship?
No. Use of a new revision in an existing relationship is not automatic unless required by the contract.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What public-interest purposes do IPC Standards and Publications serve according to this page?

They serve the public interest by eliminating misunderstandings between manufacturers and purchasers, facilitating interchangeability and improvement of products, and assisting purchasers in selecting and obtaining the proper product with minimum delay.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
If there is a conflict between the English language version and translated versions of this standard, which version takes precedence?
The English version takes precedence.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which earlier IPC-A-610 revision is listed as being published in July 2014?
IPC-A-610F.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
When was IPC-A-610 adopted for use by the Department of Defense (DoD)?
IPC-A-610 was adopted for use by the DoD on 12-FEB-02.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
To whom must proposed changes by DoD activities be submitted?
They must be submitted to the DoD Adopting Activity: Commander, US Army Tank-Automotive and Armaments Command, ATTN: AMSTA-TR-E/IE, Warren, MI 48397-5000.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which IPC committee is explicitly named on this page under the acknowledgment section?
The Product Assurance Committee is explicitly named.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Who is listed as the Technical Liaison of the IPC Board of Directors on this page?
Robert Fornefeld of L-3 Technologies is listed with the title Technical Liaison of the IPC Board of Directors.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which subsection in this section addresses acceptance criteria?
Subsection 1.5.1 addresses Acceptance Criteria.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which two inspection methodology subtopics are listed under section 1.12?
The two listed subtopics are 1.12.1 Lighting and 1.12.2 Magnification Aids.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, according to Table 1-1, which specification number is listed for the Process Requirement Standard?
The Process Requirement Standard is listed as J-STD-001.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Does IPC-A-610G provide criteria for cross-section evaluation?
No. The standard states that it does not provide criteria for cross-section evaluation.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is IPC-AJ-820 described as in relation to IPC-A-610?

IPC-AJ-820 is a supporting document that explains the intent of the specification content, amplifies the technical rationale for transition of limits through Target to Defect criteria, and provides broader understanding of related process considerations not commonly distinguishable through visual assessment.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
If there is a discrepancy between the written criteria and the illustrations in IPC-A-610G, which takes precedence?
The description or written criteria always takes precedence over the illustrations.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Who has the ultimate responsibility for identifying the class to which an assembly is evaluated?
The customer (User) has the ultimate responsibility for identifying the evaluation class. If the User does not establish and document the acceptance class, the Manufacturer may do so.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What does IPC-A-610G mean by an "Acceptable" condition?
An acceptable condition is one that, while not necessarily perfect, will maintain the integrity and reliability of the assembly in its service environment.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
If a condition is a defect for Class 1, how does that apply to Classes 2 and 3?

A defect for Class 1 automatically implies a defect for Class 2 and Class 3. Likewise, a defect for Class 2 implies a defect for Class 3, except where criteria for a particular class have not been established.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
When IPC-A-610 is cited as a stand-alone document for inspection or acceptance, do the requirements of IPC J-STD-001 apply automatically?
No. If IPC-A-610 is cited or required by contract as a stand-alone document, IPC J-STD-001 does not apply unless it is separately and specifically required.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the solder source side of a PCB, and which board side is it normally during wave, dip, or drag soldering?
The solder source side is the side of the PCB to which solder is applied. It is normally the secondary side of the PCB when wave, dip, or drag soldering are used.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is a cold solder connection according to IPC-A-610G?
A cold solder connection is a solder connection that exhibits poor wetting and is characterized by a grayish porous appearance.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What causes are listed for a cold solder connection?
The listed causes are excessive impurities in the solder, inadequate cleaning prior to soldering, and/or insufficient application of heat during the soldering process.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
According to IPC-A-610G, how are missing hardware or components classified for product classes?
Missing hardware or components are a Defect for all classes.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Who determines the class of the assembly under inspection, and what must be provided to the inspector?
The inspector does not select the class. Documentation specifying the applicable class for the assembly under inspection shall be provided to the inspector.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what maximum referee magnification is listed for wire sizes from 14 to 22 AWG?
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, which listed application says magnification is not required, with a reference to Note 1?
Cleanliness (with or without cleaning processes).
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, according to the table, what inspection magnification range is specified for land widths or land diameters greater than 0.5 mm and less than or equal to 1 mm?
3X to 7.5X.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the maximum referee magnification for land widths or land diameters smaller than 0.25 mm [0.01 in]?
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What does Note 2 in Table 1-4 say about magnification used for conformal coating/encapsulation, staking, or marking?
If magnification is used, it is limited to 4X maximum.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which IPC document listed in section 2.1 provides the acceptability criteria for printed boards?
IPC-A-600, Acceptability of Printed Boards.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which joint industry document listed in section 2.2 covers requirements for soldered electrical and electronic assemblies?
J-STD-001, Requirements for Soldered Electrical and Electronic Assemblies.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which JEDEC document is listed for symbols and labels for electrostatic sensitive devices?
JEDEC JESD471, Symbol and Label for Electrostatic Sensitive Devices.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What military standard is listed for electrostatic discharge control programs protecting electrical and electronic parts, assemblies, and equipment?
MIL-STD-1686, Electrostatic Discharge Control Program For Protection Of Electrical And Electronic Parts, Assemblies And Equipment (Excluding Electrically Initiated Explosive Devices).
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which additional documents are cited for more general information on EOS/ESD and handling considerations?
Additional information can be found in ANSI/ESD-S-20.20, IEC-61340-5, MIL-STD-1686, and other related documents.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What handling-related subsection is identified in this section overview?
The handling-related subsection is 3.3 Handling Considerations.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) according to IPC-A-610G?

ESD is the rapid transfer of a static electric charge from one object to another of a different potential, created from electrostatic sources. If that charge contacts or comes close to a sensitive component, it can damage the component.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Where should ESDS items be removed from their protective enclosures?
They should be removed only at EOS/ESD safe workstations within Electrostatic Protected Areas (EPA).
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
According to the text, what spike voltage limit is required for an increasing number of extremely sensitive components?
For an increasing number of extremely sensitive components, soldering irons, solder extractors, test instruments and other equipment must never generate spikes greater than 0.3 volt.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
When evaluating product susceptibility to EOS, which component's susceptibility should be kept in mind?
The susceptibility of the most sensitive component in the assembly should be considered.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, which packaging and handling materials are listed as typical static charge sources?
Plastic bags, wraps, envelopes; bubble wrap, foam; Styrofoam; and non-ESD totes, trays, boxes, and parts bins.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>According to the table In the provided image, what static voltage is generated by picking up a plastic bag from the bench at 10-20% humidity and at 65-90% humidity?
20,000 volts at 10-20% humidity and 1,200 volts at 65-90% humidity.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is described as the best ESD damage prevention method?
The best ESD damage prevention is a combination of preventing static charges and eliminating static charges if they do occur.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Can electrostatic discharges that are too low to be felt by humans still damage ESDS components?
Yes. Electrostatic discharges less than about 3500 volts may be too low to be felt by humans and still damage ESDS components.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, which ESD-related symbol is shown: the susceptibility symbol or the protective symbol?
Image 0 shows the ESD protective symbol.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what visual feature identifies the symbol as the ESD susceptibility symbol?
The hand inside the triangle has a slash across it, which identifies it as the ESD susceptibility symbol.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What does the ESD susceptibility symbol indicate?
It indicates that an electrical or electronic device or assembly is susceptible to damage from an ESD event.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What should be done if there is doubt about whether an assembly is ESD sensitive?
It must be handled as a sensitive device until it is determined otherwise.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What type of packaging prevents an electrostatic discharge from passing through the package into the assembly?
Static shielding packaging prevents an electrostatic discharge from passing through the package and into the assembly, helping avoid damage.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Where must ESDS items be removed from their protective enclosures?
ESDS items must be removed from their protective enclosures only at static safe workstations.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>According to the table In the provided image, what are the maximum tolerable resistance and maximum acceptable discharge time for a wrist strap to ground?
For a wrist strap to ground, the maximum tolerable resistance is 35 Megohms and the maximum acceptable discharge time is less than 0.1 sec.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What must an ESD safe workstation/EPA provide to neutralize static charges that might otherwise discharge to a device or assembly?
It must provide a path-to-ground to neutralize static charges.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
How is worker protection from live circuitry commonly accomplished in the grounding system at an EOS/ESD safe workstation?
It is commonly accomplished through resistance in line with the ground path, which also slows the charge decay time to prevent sparks or surges of energy from ESD sources.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, is the wrist strap connection shown as a parallel connected wrist strap or a series connected wrist strap?
It is shown as a parallel connected wrist strap.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what type of acceptable workstation grounding example is illustrated?
It illustrates a series connected wrist strap workstation.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What kinds of materials should be kept away from EOS/ESD workstations to reduce static generation?
Workstations should be kept free of static generating materials such as Styrofoam, plastic solder removers, sheet protectors, plastic or paper notebook folders, and employees' personal items.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What outlet type is highly recommended at EOS/ESD workstations/EPAs because of possible third-wire ground potential issues?
Ground fault interrupter (GFI) electrical outlets are highly recommended.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What handling practice is recommended for solderable surfaces to avoid solderability and corrosion problems?

Do not handle solderable surfaces with bare hands or fingers. The text states that body oils and salts reduce solderability, promote corrosion and dendritic growth, and can also cause poor adhesion of subsequent coatings or encapsulants.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What does the standard recommend if the moisture level of a PCB is unknown?

If the level of moisture is unknown, the PCB should be baked to reduce internal moisture content, with baking temperature and duration controlled to avoid reducing solderability or causing internal damage.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What kinds of physical damage can improper handling cause to components and assemblies?

Improper handling can cause cracked, chipped, or broken components and connectors, bent or broken terminals, and badly scratched board surfaces and conductor lands. This type of physical damage can ruin the entire assembly or attached components.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
According to the handling considerations for contamination, what practices help reduce contamination of boards during processing?

To reduce contamination, hands should be washed frequently, boards should be handled only by the edges without touching lands or pads, and when required, pallets and carriers should be used during processing. If gloves or finger cots are used, they should be carefully chosen and replaced often.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
How should an assembly be handled if it has no ESDS markings?
It should still be handled as if it were an ESDS assembly.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What handling precautions are recommended after soldering and cleaning to avoid contamination of electronic assemblies?

Electronic assemblies should be handled with great care; gloves or other protective handling devices may be used to prevent contamination such as fingerprints, and mechanical racking or baskets with full ESD protection should be used during cleaning operations.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, is the printed circuit board being held with bare hands or with gloves?
It is being held with bare hands, not gloves.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what handling method is shown for the printed circuit board?
The board is being handled with gloved hands.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Why may gloves or finger cots be required when handling parts and assemblies?
They may be required under contract to prevent contamination of parts and assemblies.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What can happen if an assembly related component is handled without EOS/ESD protection?
It may damage electrostatic sensitive components, causing latent failures, product degradation not detectable during initial test, or catastrophic failures found at initial test.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What kinds of hardware attachments are covered in this section for mounting devices to a printed circuit assembly?

This section covers hardware such as screws, bolts, nuts, washers, fasteners, clips, component studs, tie downs, rivets, connector pins, and similar items used to mount devices to a PCA or other assemblies.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Do the criteria in this section apply to attachments with self-tapping screws?
No. The criteria in this section do not apply to attachments with self-tapping screws.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, does the metallic hardware meet or violate the electrical clearance requirement?
It violates the requirement; the metallic hardware reduces the spacing to less than the specified minimum electrical clearance, so it is a defect.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, is the hardware installation acceptable with respect to electrical clearance?
Yes. The installation is acceptable because the spacing between the metallic hardware and the noncommon conductors meets the specified minimum electrical clearance.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
For which IPC classes is hardware installation acceptable when spacing between noncommon conductors does not violate the specified minimum electrical clearance?
It is acceptable for Class 1, 2, and 3.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the defect condition described for hardware installation electrical clearance?
A defect occurs when hardware reduces spacing to less than the specified minimum electrical clearance.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, does the mounting hole appear clear enough to support the acceptable hardware installation interference condition?
Yes. The mounting hole appears open and unobstructed, which matches the acceptable condition that the mounting area be clear of obstructions to assembly requirements.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
For hardware installation interference, what is acceptable for Class 1, 2, and 3?
The mounting area shall be clear of obstructions to assembly requirements.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Under hardware installation interference, what conditions are defects for Class 1, 2, and 3?
Defects include excess solder (uneven) on mounting holes where mechanical assembly will be affected, and anything that interferes with mounting of required hardware.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, which numbered part is the heat sink?
The heat sink is part 3.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, which numbered callout identifies the terminal lug attached near the component case?
The terminal lug is callout 2.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the acceptability classification for the high power component mounting hardware installation shown on this page?
It is acceptable for Class 1, Class 2, and Class 3.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
According to the text for Figure 4-5, where is a thermal conductor placed when specified?
It is placed between the mating surfaces of the power device and the heat sink.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, which numbered feature is identified as the sharp edge of the washer against the insulator?
Feature ① is the sharp edge of the washer against the insulator.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, where is the lock washer located relative to the terminal lug and component case?
The lock washer is positioned between the terminal lug and the component case.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Under what section is the hardware installation content on this page categorized?
It is under section 4.1.3, Hardware Installation - Component Mounting - High Power (cont.).
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
According to the text on this page, for which IPC classes is the condition identified as a defect?
It is a defect for Class 1, Class 2, and Class 3.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What does visual inspection include for heatsink hardware installation?
Visual inspection includes checking hardware security, component damage, and the correct sequence of assembly.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What may be specified in place of hardware for heatsink mounting?
Bonding with thermally conductive adhesives may be specified in place of hardware.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, is the visible border around the component uniform or non-uniform, and how is it classified?
The border is non-uniform, and it is acceptable for Class 1, 2, and 3 because evidence of the material is still visible around the component edges.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, does the heatsink installation show the target condition or only an acceptable condition for the insulator/thermal compound border?
It shows the target condition, because a uniform border is visible around the edges of the component.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, is there visible evidence of mica, plastic film, or thermal compound showing around the component edges?
No. There is no visible border showing around the component edges, which corresponds to a defect condition for Class 1, 2, and 3 in this figure sequence.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the target condition for heatsink insulators or thermal compounds around the edges of the component?
For Class 1, 2, and 3, the target condition is a uniform border of mica, plastic film, or thermal compound showing around the edges of the component.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the acceptable condition for heatsink insulators or thermal compounds around the component edges?
For Class 1, 2, and 3, it is acceptable if the border is not uniform but there is still evidence of mica, plastic film, or thermal compound showing around the edges of the component.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what defect is shown at the heatsink contact area marked A?
A visible separation is present between the heatsink and the contact layer at A, indicating defective heatsink contact for Class 1, 2, and 3.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what installation condition is illustrated for the heatsink contact area marked A?
It shows the target condition for Class 1, 2, and 3, with the heatsink seated flush on the contact layer at A and no visible gap.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what feature near A makes this heatsink installation acceptable?
A small localized gap is visible near the left side of the heatsink contact area at A, but the figure is identified as acceptable for Class 1, 2, and 3.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What hardware installation topic is covered in section 4.1.4.2 on this page?
Hardware Installation - Heatsinks - Contact.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Which IPC classes are listed for the target, acceptable, and defect conditions on this page?
Class 1, 2, and 3 are listed for all three conditions.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what hardware sequence and orientation is shown beneath the solder lug?
The image shows a flat washer directly under the solder lug, with the lock washer below it and oriented so its sharp edge faces toward the flat washer.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what are the parts labeled 1 through 4 in the hardware stack-up?
1 is the lock washer with its sharp edge showing toward the flat washer, 2 is the flat washer, 3 is nonconductive material (laminate, etc.), and 4 is metal (not conductive pattern or foil).
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
Unless otherwise specified, which way should the sharp edges of a lock washer face during hardware installation?
The sharp edges of the lock washer should be against the flat washer.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
What is the class acceptability when less than one and one-half threads extend beyond the threaded hardware unless otherwise specified by the engineering drawing?
It is acceptable for Class 1 and a defect for Class 2 and 3.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, which numbered part is the flat washer installed with the lock washer under the fastener head?
Part 3 is the flat washer.
You are an expert in PCBA visual inspection and manufacturing standards. Answer the factual question concisely using the provided IPC-A-610 standard content.
<image>In the provided image, what material is identified by callout 2 beneath the lock washer?
